name: Mirkwood
description: The great wood east of the Anduin, under the long shadow of Dol Guldur. The canopy mutes the sky — rain reaches the forest floor as dripping for hours after the storm has passed, the sun is a rumour through tangled branches, and the air itself seems thicker than it has any right to be. Weather under the eaves is less a matter of season than of mood.

spring.label: The canopy gives back only a dim green light, the air still and heavy.
Water dripping from every leaf — no rain, only the forest remembering rain.
Low mist among the trunks, the path lost beyond a few paces.
Shafts of sunlight pierce the canopy here and there — a good day by Mirkwood's measure.
Rain falling somewhere above; down here a steady dim patter and darker trees.
Thick fog in the lower places, the trees ghosting in and out of view.
A high wind moving through the canopy — the forest floor still, the crowns creaking.
Distant thunder rolling above the trees — flashes of light through the leaves, no rain reaching below.

summer.label: Still, green, stifling — the air thick enough to taste, not a leaf moving.
Dim and humid, sweat running freely, every surface damp to the touch.
Warm mist clinging to the trunks, beads of water on every cobweb.
A slow dripping from the canopy, hours after any rain has ended.
Shafts of gold breaking through — in these places the forest almost feels kind.
Thunder above the canopy, muted and rolling — no rain reaches the floor.
Rain heavy enough to reach the floor — a dim steady fall, the paths running muddy.
A stillness that feels wrong — no birds, no insects, only the weight of the wood.

autumn.label: Cold mist among the trunks, leaves falling slow and silent through it.
Dim and raw under the canopy, the damp working through every layer.
Steady cold rain finding its way through the thinning leaves.
Cold dripping from every branch, the forest floor a slick mat of wet leaves.
A hard wind in the canopy, branches creaking, dead wood falling unseen.
Heavy fog reducing the world to a few yards of wet trunk and wetter leaf.
A brief fair spell, the wood briefly lit russet and gold — it does not last.
A stillness in which the trees seem to lean close — something watches from nearby.

winter.label: Bitterly cold under the canopy, the cold held in by the tangle of black branches.
Hoarfrost on every branch and hanging web — the forest in rime-grey.
Freezing fog among the trunks, the air itself seeming to bite.
Little snow reaches the floor — only strange patches where the canopy breaks.
A still black cold, no wind, no sound — only breath steaming in the dim light.
A wind shrieking in the high branches, nothing stirring on the floor below.
Freezing rain finding gaps in the canopy — every surface turning to glass.
A cold that feels intent, that feels sent — the Shadow thick under the eaves tonight.
